text.en: Your school is organizing a massive health awareness week.
I volunteer to run the CPR and first-aid demonstration booth.
I paint the colorful banners and posters to promote the event.
I manage the budget and find local sponsors to fund the activities.
I ensure all physical activities comply with the school's safety regulations.
I build the physical framework for the interactive displays.
The student council wants to revamp the old, unused courtyard.
I sketch a beautiful layout for a new mural and seating area.
I calculate the costs and run a fundraiser to pay for materials.
I write a proposal to the principal arguing why students deserve this space.
I design a sturdy, weather-proof bench and table system.
I survey the students to see what kind of space would make them happiest.
Your town is hosting a massive weekend flea market and you want to participate.
I set up a stall to sell my old video games and maximize my profits.
I read the market guidelines to make sure our booth meets all vendor rules.
I construct a clever folding table that is easy to transport and set up.
I spend my time chatting with other vendors and making new friends.
I focus on selling upcycled items to reduce waste.
Your science class is doing a project on the local river's water quality.
I debate the town's water policies and argue for stricter pollution controls.
I build a custom water filtration device to test on the samples.
I organize a weekend student cleanup crew for the riverbanks.
I study how the local fish and plant life are being affected.
I spend hours in the library looking up historical water data for comparison.
Your neighborhood is completely snowed in after a massive blizzard.
I rig up a makeshift snowplow using my bike and some scrap wood.
I go door-to-door to check if the elderly neighbors need anything.
I make sure the local birds and wildlife have access to food.
I look up meteorological maps to track when the storm will finally pass.
I organize a massive neighborhood snowball fight and hot cocoa party.
Your school requires everyone to complete 10 hours of community service.
I volunteer at the local youth center tutoring younger kids.
I join the tree-planting initiative at the community park.
I analyze demographic data for a local non-profit to help target campaigns.
I host a loud and lively charity auction in the school gym.
I silently organize the inventory in the back room of a food bank.
Your biology teacher assigns a massive end-of-year group presentation.
I choose to focus our topic on the effects of climate change on local bugs.
I dive deep into the academic journals to find the most accurate facts.
I volunteer to be the main speaker who presents in front of the whole class.
I handle creating the slides quietly by myself so they look perfect.
I create a strict timeline so our group doesn't leave anything to the last minute.
You are placed in a debate tournament, and your team is given a tough topic.
I dig up obscure statistics and facts to build an unbreakable argument.
I take the lead on the stage, using my charisma to sway the judges.
I sit back and write down rebuttals for my teammates to use.
I format our notes meticulously so we can find any point in seconds.
When the other team attacks our points, I stay completely calm and collected.
Your family is planning a two-week summer road trip.
I convince everyone we should stop at the busiest tourist traps and theme parks.
I pack my headphones and a good book to enjoy the quiet time in the backseat.
I map out the exact route, gas stops, and hotel reservations.
When the car gets a flat tire, I keep everyone from panicking.
I suggest we ditch the plan and explore a weird, unknown roadside attraction.
You're trying to build a complicated 1000-piece puzzle but a piece seems to be missing.
I sit alone in my room for hours, hyper-focused on scanning every piece.
I sort all the pieces by color and edge type into neat little piles.
I don't get frustrated; I just calmly keep working on the rest of the puzzle.
I decide to paint a tiny piece of cardboard to match the missing spot.
I deduce exactly what shape and color the missing piece must be based on the gap.
Your group is locked in an escape room with only 10 minutes left.
I systematically check every drawer and corner we might have missed.
I tell everyone to take a deep breath and stop yelling.
I try completely ridiculous ideas, like reading the clues backward.
I crack the math code on the final padlock by finding the pattern.
I coordinate everyone so we are all searching different parts of the room.
During a school play, the main prop breaks right before the curtain opens.
I calm down the panicking lead actor and tell them it will be fine.
I suggest we completely change the scene to make the broken prop part of the joke.
I quickly duct-tape and wire the prop back together so it holds up.
I grab three stagehands and we work together to swap it for a backup.
I analyze which scenes need the prop and figure out if we can just skip it.
Your teacher asks you to submit a final project in any format you want.
I decide to do an interpretive dance instead of a standard essay.
I build a functional mechanical model to demonstrate the concepts.
I team up with two classmates to make a comprehensive documentary.
I write an essay that challenges the core assumptions of the textbook.
I design an original board game that teaches the subject to players.
Your school's WiFi goes down right when you have an online assignment due.
I figure out how to tether my phone's data to my laptop to get it submitted.
I text my classmates to see who has a connection and if we can share a hotspot.
I realize the due date will likely be extended and prioritize offline studying instead.
I draw my assignment by hand and take a photo to submit later.
I quickly learn how to use a new offline app to finish the work.
You want to start a podcast about high school life with your friends.
I make sure everyone gets equal talking time and we brainstorm topics together.
I listen to our test recordings and analyze what makes them engaging or boring.
I come up with a highly unique theme and catchy name for our show.
I watch a few tutorials and immediately figure out how to mix the audio.
I use an AI tool to clean up our background noise and generate show notes.
You're tasked with organizing the school's talent show.
I review past shows to figure out why some acts dragged and fix the schedule.
I come up with a spectacular new lighting concept for the stage.
I quickly master the school's old soundboard after playing with it for an hour.
I use an AI scheduling assistant to coordinate 30 different student acts.
I set up a multi-camera livestream so parents can watch from home.
You notice a lot of students are completely stressed out during exam week.
I design a 'smash room' with bubble wrap for students to relieve stress.
I rapidly read up on stress-relief techniques and share the best ones.
I build an AI chatbot that sends encouraging messages and study reminders.
I code an app that tracks sleep and study hours to prevent burnout.
I organize a healthy snack station and a meditation circle in the library.
You want to learn how to make your own video game.
I jump straight into a new coding language and pick it up as I go.
I use AI image generators to quickly create the background assets.
I focus on the core programming logic and debugging the game engine.
I design a game that encourages players to exercise and eat well.
I spend most of my time drawing the character sprites and composing the music.
Your friend wants to start a YouTube channel about fitness and nutrition.
I use AI to write SEO-optimized video titles and descriptions.
I handle the camera equipment, lighting, and video editing software.
I help them research accurate nutritional information for the videos.
I design the thumbnails and create the channel's visual branding.
I figure out how to monetize the channel and get local gym sponsorships.
The school cafeteria is trying to improve its daily lunch menu.
I create a digital voting system where students can rate the meals.
I advocate for more fresh vegetables and balanced macros.
I make the cafeteria look more inviting with new signs and menu boards.
I calculate how to offer better food without raising the lunch prices.
I check the state nutritional guidelines to ensure our new menu is compliant.
Your school is hosting a massive track and field sports day.
I volunteer at the injury tent, handing out ice packs and bandages.
I design the logos and paint the faces of everyone on my team.
I run the snack stand and maximize our profits for the sports club.
I act as a referee to ensure no one is breaking the tournament rules.
I fix the broken hurdles and measure the long jump sandpit accurately.
The school library is undergoing a complete makeover.
I arrange the reading nooks with aesthetically pleasing colors and lighting.
I organize a used book sale to help buy new comfy chairs.
I draft a new set of rules regarding noise levels and computer usage.
I assemble the new heavy-duty metal bookshelves.
I set up a peer reading group to help freshmen find good books.
Your class is organizing a bake sale for a local charity.
I set the prices and track every single dollar we make.
I write down all the allergy warnings so we don't violate food safety rules.
I build a tiered wooden display stand to show off the cupcakes.
I stand at the front, loudly greeting everyone who walks by.
I make sure we have plenty of vegan and ethically sourced options.
You are participating in the regional science fair.
I check the patent databases to make sure my idea hasn't been done.
I construct a working robotic arm out of spare parts.
I cheer on my classmates and help them practice their speeches.
I present a project on reducing microplastics in the ocean.
I collect hundreds of data points and organize them into clear tables.
Your community center received a donation of broken bicycles.
I weld the cracked frames and tighten the loose chains.
I teach the younger kids how to ride the bikes once they are fixed.
I recycle the ruined tires and rubber grips properly.
I read the original manufacturer manuals to find the exact specs.
I organize a massive group bike ride through town to celebrate.
You volunteer at the local animal shelter on weekends.
I spend my time talking to visitors and helping them find the right pet.
I clean the outdoor pens and plant dog-friendly grass in the yard.
I track the adoption rates and figure out which breeds are most popular.
I hype up the crowd at the shelter's public adoption events.
I sit quietly in the back room rehabilitating the shy, scared cats.
Your scout troop goes on a weekend camping trip.
I make sure we follow 'leave no trace' principles and clean up the site.
I use a guidebook to identify the different plants and tracks we see.
I tell loud ghost stories around the campfire to entertain everyone.
I wake up early to enjoy the quiet sunrise completely by myself.
I double-check everyone's packing list so we don't forget the tent pegs.
Your math team makes it to the state finals.
I study advanced college-level theorems to prepare for the hardest questions.
I lead the team cheer and high-five everyone before the test starts.
I sit in the corner with my headphones on, running practice drills alone.
I meticulously check my work line by line to avoid silly errors.
When we miss a question, I don't get upset; I just focus on the next one.
You and your friends go to a loud, chaotic music festival.
I push my way to the front row and dance wildly in the crowd.
I wear earplugs and stay near the back so I don't get overwhelmed.
I create a detailed schedule so we see all our favorite bands on time.
When it starts pouring rain, I shrug it off and keep having fun.
I ditch the main stage to find a weird, obscure indie band playing in a tent.
You get lost in the woods while hiking with your family.
I stop talking to focus entirely on listening for highway sounds.
I pull out the compass and strictly follow a straight path north.
I stay totally calm and prevent my younger siblings from panicking.
I suggest we try climbing a strange rock formation to get a better view.
I triangulate our position using the sun and the shadows.
You and your friends are cooking a fancy dinner for your parents.
I strictly follow the recipe measurements down to the gram.
When the smoke alarm goes off, I calmly turn on the fan and open a window.
I throw in some unusual spices just to see how it alters the flavor.
The sauce is too salty, so I figure out how to balance it with lemon and sugar.
I delegate chopping, stirring, and setting the table so we finish together.
You host a movie night, but the power suddenly goes out.
I tell everyone to relax and not worry while we wait it out.
I suggest we make shadow puppets on the wall with our flashlights.
I go check the breaker box to see if I can restore the power safely.
I get everyone to pitch in and clean up the snacks in the dark.
I analyze the grid map on my phone to predict when the blackout will end.
You are redesigning your bedroom to make it cooler.
I paint my ceiling black and cover it with glow-in-the-dark stars.
I build a custom shelf that perfectly fits the awkward corner.
I ask my siblings to help me move the heavy bed and dresser.
I measure the space and determine which layout maximizes walking room.
I make a unique lamp out of an old skateboard and some string lights.
Your neighbor's dog goes missing, and everyone is trying to find it.
I figure out the places the dog usually hides and check there first.
I form a search party and assign everyone a specific street to check.
I realize searching randomly won't work and create a grid map instead.
I use a recording of the owner's voice playing from a drone to lure him.
I quickly read up on animal tracking techniques to look for paw prints.
You join the school's coding and app development club.
I do pair programming, working closely with a partner on the same code.
I review other people's code to find logical errors and bugs.
I pitch a completely wild idea for an app that gamifies homework.
I learn a completely new database language in just a weekend.
I use an AI assistant to autocompleting my boilerplate code faster.
You are on the editorial team for the school newspaper.
I fact-check every article to make sure the arguments actually make sense.
I come up with a completely new layout design that looks like a magazine.
I quickly figure out how to use the complex publishing software.
I run the articles through an AI tool to check for tone and clarity.
I manage the newspaper's website and fix the broken image links.
Your school is launching an anti-bullying campaign.
I direct an emotional, creative skit to be performed at the assembly.
I take a quick course on peer mediation and start applying the techniques.
I use AI sentiment analysis on school forums to spot toxic trends.
I code an anonymous reporting app for students to use safely.
I bring in therapy dogs to comfort students who have been bullied.
You are part of the school's drama production.
I memorize all my lines and blocking in record time.
I use an AI prompt to generate extra dialogue for the background characters.
I program the complicated lighting rig to sync perfectly with the music.
I remind the cast to drink tea and do vocal warm-ups to protect their throats.
I spend hours painting the intricate backdrops for the castle scene.
You and your friends start a tech entrepreneurship club.
I use AI to generate a business plan and market analysis in minutes.
I set up the club's servers and build our actual prototype website.
I make sure we have ergonomic chairs to prevent back pain during hackathons.
I design a slick, professional logo for our startup.
I prepare the pitch deck to secure funding from local businesses.
Your school's e-sports team is preparing for a major tournament.
I optimize our PCs and test the ping to ensure we have zero lag.
I lead the team in wrist stretches to prevent carpal tunnel syndrome.
I create the animated overlays for our Twitch stream.
I secure sponsorships with gaming gear companies to pay for our entry fees.
I read the tournament rulebook carefully to make sure we don't get disqualified.
You are starting a First Responder and First Aid club at school.
I demonstrate how to use a defibrillator and do chest compressions.
I use makeup to create realistic fake wounds for our practice drills.
I collect the club dues to buy more bandages and medical supplies.
I draft the liability waiver forms that members have to sign.
I build a custom splinting device out of 3D-printed plastic.
You are working on the senior yearbook committee.
I design the front cover and pick the aesthetic theme for the pages.
I sell ad space to local restaurants to lower the cost of the yearbook.
I review the senior quotes to ensure none violate the school's code of conduct.
I fix the broken binding machine we use to assemble the drafts.
I interview the shy students to make sure everyone gets featured.
You are helping run a pop-up thrift store in the gymnasium.
I decide the pricing tiers and manage the cash box.
I make sure we charge the correct sales tax according to local laws.
I assemble the heavy metal clothing racks so they don't tip over.
I act as a personal shopper, helping students pick out cool outfits.
I only accept donations of eco-friendly and biodegradable fabrics.
Your class is doing a mock trial about a factory polluting a town.
I play the lead attorney and cross-examine the witnesses aggressively.
I build a scale model of the factory to use as physical evidence.
I work with my team to make sure everyone feels heard during deliberations.
I focus my arguments on the severe damage done to the local ecosystem.
I read past Supreme Court cases to find precedents for our argument.
Your school's robotics team is building a battle bot.
I solder the wires and assemble the bot's titanium armor.
I host team dinners to make sure everyone bonds and gets along.
I scavenge old electronics from the dump to reuse their motors.
I analyze the designs of past champions to see what weapons work best.
I get on the microphone and hype up the crowd before our match.
You go on a summer volunteer trip to a remote village.
I spend all day playing soccer and games with the local kids.
I help plant drought-resistant crops in the community garden.
I interview the elders to document the village's oral history.
I perform a song at the massive town festival.
I spend my evenings quietly journaling and reflecting by myself.
You organize a massive beach cleanup on Saturday morning.
I focus on safely separating the microplastics from the seaweed.
I check the tide charts to determine the absolute safest time to clean.
I run around with a megaphone directing the large crowds of volunteers.
I walk far down the shoreline so I can pick up trash completely alone.
I bring a meticulous checklist and sorting bags for different types of waste.
You are competing in the regional chess club championship.
I study hundreds of grandmaster games to memorize opening theory.
I confidently walk around the hall chatting and psyching out opponents.
I wear noise-canceling headphones to block everyone out during my match.
I carefully write down every single move in my notebook without failing.
When I make a huge blunder, I don't panic; I just keep playing my best.
You go to a massive, crowded theme park for a friend's birthday.
I scream and throw my hands up in the very front row of the roller coaster.
I avoid the crowds by riding the slow, quiet observation wheel.
I plan out our fast-passes so we never waste time walking back and forth.
When the ride breaks down while we are on it, I just relax and wait.
I drag everyone to a weird, neon-lit 4D experience nobody has heard of.
You are accidentally locked out of your house without your keys.
I sit quietly on the porch reading a book on my phone until someone gets home.
I meticulously retrace my steps to see if I dropped the keys in the grass.
I don't freak out or get angry; I just accept the situation.
I try getting in through the dog door or climbing a tree to a balcony.
I figure out how to pop the garage door latch using a wire hanger.
